{{Quote|What about the analogy to the Fw'Sen? Don't they mate only once, before they're sexually mature?|[[Hiram Drayson/Legends|Hiram Drayson]]|<ref name="TT" />}}
The '''Fw'Sen''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] that [[Reproduction/Legends|mated]] only once, prior to being sexually mature, and retained the resulting fertilized [[Egg/Legends|eggs]] within their bodies until a later stage in their reproductive cycle. The [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Ssi-ruu]]k designed and produced a [[Fw'Sen-class picket ship|''Fw'Sen''-class picket ship]].

==Behavior==
The Fw'Sen were a [[Species/Legends|species]] notable for [[Reproduction/Legends|mating]] once before they were sexually mature. The resulting fertilized [[Egg/Legends|eggs]] were then retained within their bodies until the Fw'Sen reached sexual maturity, at which point the reproductive process continued.<ref name="TT" />

==History==
The [[Ssi-ruu]]k, a [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species from the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], named one of their [[Warship/Legends|warship]] designs the [[Fw'Sen-class picket ship|''Fw'Sen''-class picket ship]]. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], twenty of these [[Starship/Legends|ships]]<ref name="TAB">''[[The Truce at Bakura]]''</ref>—manned solely by [[P'w'eck]]s, a Ssi-Ruuk [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] species<ref name="The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook">''[[The Truce at Bakura Sourcebook]]''</ref>—were part of the taskforce that [[Invasion of Bakura|assaulted]] the [[Bakura system/Legends|Bakura system]], before being defeated by a combined [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]]–[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] force.<ref name="TAB" />

Later, [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Hiram Drayson/Legends|Hiram Drayson]] was familiar with the species. While trying to uncover the origins of the [[Teljkon Vagabond]]—a mysterious organic ship of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] design—in [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], Drayson suggested an analogy between the stored genetic code in the ship and the Fw'Sen mating process. However, this was dismissed by biologist [[Joi Eicroth]] as an inappropriate analogy to make.<ref name="TT" />

==Behind the scenes==
The word "Fw'Sen" first appeared in ''[[The Truce at Bakura]]'', a novel written by [[Kathy Tyers]] and published in [[1994]], in which it was part of the ''Fw'Sen''-class starship designation. It was not until the release of the novel ''[[Tyrant's Test]]'' in [[1996]] that the Fw'Sen were identified by [[Michael P. Kube-McDowell]] as a specific creature. However, he made no explicit identification with the spacecraft. While the species' name is capitalized in Kube-McDowell's work, there is no indication that it is sentient.

==Appearances==
*''[[Tyrant's Test]]'' {{1stm}}
